strategies.name: Test i32 1 for llvm.loop.vectorize.enable
strategies.rationale: The patch enforces that `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` must have exactly two operands, and the second operand must be of type `i1`. However, existing tests like `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize/disable_nonforced_enable.ll` and `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize/remove_metadata.ll` use `i32 1` for the second operand. The patch will cause the verifier to fail on these valid IRs.
strategies.expected_issue: The verifier will reject the valid IR because it expects `i1` but the IR uses `i32 1`.

strategies.name: Test i32 0 for llvm.loop.vectorize.enable
strategies.rationale: Similar to the `i32 1` case, some tests might use `i32 0` to disable vectorization. The patch enforces `i1`, which will break these tests.
strategies.expected_issue: The verifier will reject the valid IR because it expects `i1` but the IR uses `i32 0`.

strategies.name: Test two operands for llvm.loop.unroll.disable
strategies.rationale: The patch enforces that `llvm.loop.unroll.disable` must have exactly one operand. However, some tests like `llvm/test/tools/gold/X86/slp-vectorize-pm.ll` and `llvm/test/Transforms/IndVarSimplify/pr68260.ll` use `!{!"llvm.loop.unroll.disable", i1 true}`. The patch will cause the verifier to fail on these valid IRs.
strategies.expected_issue: The verifier will reject the valid IR because it expects exactly one operand but the IR uses two operands.

strategies.name: Test two operands for llvm.loop.unroll.enable
strategies.rationale: The patch enforces that `llvm.loop.unroll.enable` must have exactly one operand. If any test uses `!{!"llvm.loop.unroll.enable", i1 true}` or similar, it will be rejected.
strategies.expected_issue: The verifier will reject the valid IR because it expects exactly one operand but the IR uses two operands.

strategies.name: Test two operands for llvm.loop.unroll.full
strategies.rationale: The patch enforces that `llvm.loop.unroll.full` must have exactly one operand. If any test uses `!{!"llvm.loop.unroll.full", i1 true}` or similar, it will be rejected.
strategies.expected_issue: The verifier will reject the valid IR because it expects exactly one operand but the IR uses two operands.

reason_thou:
Fix Understanding:
The patch adds a new function `verifyLoopMetadata` to the `Verifier` class. This function checks the syntax of loop metadata nodes (`!llvm.loop`).
Specifically, it checks:
1. `llvm.loop.distribute.enable` and `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` must have exactly two operands: the name and an `i1` value.
2. `llvm.loop.unroll.disable`, `llvm.loop.unroll.enable`, `llvm.loop.unroll.full`, `llvm.loop.unroll_and_jam.enable`, and `llvm.loop.unroll_and_jam.disable` must have exactly one operand: just the name.

Formal Semantic Modeling:
Before the fix: The verifier did not check the syntax of these specific loop metadata nodes. Any number of operands and any types were allowed.
After the fix: The verifier strictly enforces the number of operands and the type of the second operand (for boolean attributes).
Conditions for difference: If existing IR uses a different type for the boolean value (e.g., `i32 1` instead of `i1 true`) or provides a boolean value for the single-operand attributes (e.g., `!{!"llvm.loop.unroll.disable", i1 true}`), the verifier will now reject it.

Assumptions and Preconditions Identified:
1. The patch assumes that all valid IR uses `i1` for the boolean value of `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable` and `llvm.loop.distribute.enable`.
2. The patch assumes that all valid IR uses exactly one operand for `llvm.loop.unroll.disable`, `llvm.loop.unroll.enable`, etc.

Potential Cases to Break Assumptions:
1. I found that `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize/disable_nonforced_enable.ll` uses `!{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i32 1}`. This breaks assumption 1.
2. I found that `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize/remove_metadata.ll` uses `!{!"llvm.loop.vectorize.enable", i32 1}`. This breaks assumption 1.
3. I found that `llvm/test/Transforms/IndVarSimplify/pr68260.ll` uses `!{!"llvm.loop.unroll.disable", i1 true}`. This breaks assumption 2.
4. I found that `llvm/test/tools/gold/X86/slp-vectorize-pm.ll` uses `!{!"llvm.loop.unroll.disable", i1 true}`. This breaks assumption 2.

Test Strategies:
1. Test `i32 1` for `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable`.
2. Test `i32 0` for `llvm.loop.vectorize.enable`.
3. Test `i1 true` for `llvm.loop.unroll.disable`.
4. Test `i1 true` for `llvm.loop.unroll.enable`.
5. Test `i1 true` for `llvm.loop.unroll.full`.
